        'float': ctypes.c_float,
        'double': ctypes.c_double,
        'void': ctypes.c_void_p,
    }

    def __init__(self, name, type_str, size=None, alignment=None):
        self.name = name
        self.type_str = type_str
        self.size = size
        self.alignment = alignment
        self.primitive_type = self.PRIMITIVE_TYPES.get(type_str, None)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Type(name='{self.name}', type_str='{self.type_str}', size={self.size}, alignment={self.alignment})"

# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Create a type object for a 32-bit integer
    int_type = Type('int', 'int', size=4, alignment=4)
    print(int_type)

    # Create a type object for a custom struct
    struct_type = Type('MyStruct', 'struct', size=16, alignment=8)
    print(struct_type)
```

def get_events():
    events = []
    for i in range(1, 10):
        date = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        title = f"Event {i}"
        text = f"This is the text for event {i}."
        url = f"https://example.com/event{i}"
        image = f"https://example.com/image{i}.jpg"
        tags = ["tag1", "tag2", "tag3"]
        event = {
            "date": date,
            "rev": i, 
            "title": title,
            "text": text,
            "url": url,
            "image": image,
            "tags": tags
        }
        events.append(event)
    return events

def get_event(i):
    date = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    title = f"Event {i}"
    text = f"This is the text for event {i}."
    url = f"https://example.com/event{i}"
    image = f"https://example.com/image{i}.jpg"

name: dict_config_real_039
text:
# Assuming this is a custom comparison
        "!=": lambda x, y: x != y,
        ">=": lambda x, y: x >= y,
        "<=": lambda x, y: x <= y,
        ">": lambda x, y: x > y,
        "<": lambda x, y: x < y,
    }

    # Check if the operation is valid
    if operation not in operations:
        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported operation: {operation}")

    # Perform the operation
    result = operations[operation](value, threshold)

    return result

# Example usage:
print(check_threshold(10, 5, "=="))  # Output: True
print(check_threshold(10, 5, ">="))  # Output: True
print(check_threshold(10, 15, "<"))  # Output: True
print(check_threshold(10, 15, "!=")) # Output: True
print(check_threshold(10, 10, "~=")) # Output: True
print(check_threshold(10, 12, ">"))  # Output: False
print(check_threshold(10, 12, "<=")) # Output: False
print
